data.title: The Southern Company Q4 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
The Southern Company (SO) will report its results for the fourth quarter on Thursday, before market open.

Wall Street expects the company to post earnings per share of $0.56, implying a rise of 12% year over year, on a revenue of $6.3 billion.

During the quarter, Southern Company raised $2 billion through an equity units offering to refinance debt, repay short-term obligations and support corporate needs, while analysts adopted a more cautious stance following regulatory changes in Georgia that heightened uncertainty around future rate increases.

Its subsidiary Georgia Power advanced new generation capacity plans, received major turbine components for expansion at Plant Yates, and reached an agreement with the Georgia Public Service Commission aimed at lowering typical residential customer costs as electricity demand from large-load users continued to grow. Earlier in the period, the utility reported stronger commercial and industrial power usage, reflecting rising electricity demand from businesses.

According to Alpha’s Quant Rating system, SO is rated Hold with an overall score of 3.22 out of 5, reflecting grades of A in terms of profitability but a D in momentum.

A Seeking Alpha analyst downgraded the stock to Hold, citing valuation alignment after a strong share price rally and neutral technical conditions, noting that “the company is solid, but the valuation is not aligned with the stock price after a strong rally since the start of 2025.”

Over the last two years, SO has beaten EPS estimates 88% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 50% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen no upward revisions and six downward revisions, while revenue estimates have seen three upward revisions and one downward revision.

data.title: BofA highlights Microsoft, Meta's contribution to Arista's 2025 revenue
data.description:
BofA said Arista's 10-K filing indicated that Microsoft (MSFT) accounted for 26% of the company's total 2025 revenues, while Meta Platforms (META) accounted for 16%, and highlighted implications of the contributions.

BofA kept its Buy rating and $185 price target on Arista's stock.

Analysts led by Tal Liani said they previously assumed the opposite contribution between the two, given Microsoft's delay in migrating from InfiniBand to Ethernet for AI back-end networks.

The analysts noted that Microsoft did not yet deploy Ethernet at scale for AI back-end networks in 2025 with only less than 100K graphics processing unit, or GPU, deployments, but still grew an impressive 67% year-over-year, or about $1B on a dollar basis.

This growth is almost purely attributed to front-end switching, which supports management’s claims for strong pull through from back-end buildouts, according to the analysts.

"This bodes well for Arista in 2026, as Microsoft ramps its Ethernet back-end deployments and as Arista retains meaningful share, together with Nvidia, on limited participation of white box deployments at Microsoft. We flag the disparity between the significant ramp in deferred revenues versus the slower revenue ramp, which increases the potential for large revenue recognition as Microsoft reaches certain deployment milestones at their Fairwater data centers," said Liani and his team.

White box networking refers to using unbranded, commodity hardware switches combined with independent, often open-source, network operating systems. A white box switch is a network switch that is assembled from standardized commodity parts.

The analysts noted that Meta grew 41% year-over-year in 2025, supported by large Ethernet back-end deployments, with Arista present in the Leaf and Spine layers, while Celestica and Accton are mostly in the top-of-rack layer.

Leaf and spine is a two-layer network topology designed for high-speed communication in data centers.

The analysts added that while the growth is impressive and supported by the strong capital expenditure, or capex, they believe it reflects the continued share losses at Meta, as both Nvidia (NVDA) and white box vendors gain share. At a high level, Arista has limited competition in spine switching, large clusters, and modular switches. Arista is also strong at Meta with its 7800 switch that carries routing capabilities, the analysts noted.

"However, as Meta’s FBOSS [Facebook open switching system] software improves over time, we see white boxes taking more share over time, and the recent multi-year agreement of Nvidia with Meta to supply GPUs also presents share loss risk, as Nvidia tends to dominate switching deployments of its own GPU clusters," said Liani and his team. 

The analysts noted that the market share and growth dynamics at Arista are being skewed by the significant $2.5B increase in annual deferred revenues. Liani and his team said that while they point to share losses at Meta, based on reported revenues, the real trends might be somewhat better if orders are booked as deferred revenues instead of being recognized as revenues until certain milestones are met.

"In addition, we believe share losses exist and are currently masked by the high capex tide, but we also expect 2026 to be a strong year for switching and for Arista accordingly, with revenue growth likely exceeding 30% YoY vs. management’s 25% guidance. Agentic AI is driving infrastructure spending up and the Cloud Titans are catering to future needs by building capacity ahead of time," the analysts added.

data.title: Most and least shorted energy stocks with up to $2B market cap as of mid-Feb
data.description:
By mid-February, the S&P 500 energy sector (XLE) moved 22% in the upward direction this year supported by a 13% rise in crude oil futures (CL1:COM) over potential disruption in global crude supply.

Against this backdrop, short interest trends among micro to small-cap energy stocks show a wide divergence. Sable Offshore (SOC) was the most shorted stock in mid-February, while Vantage (VNTG) 0.50% was the least shorted stock.

Most shorted energy stocks as of mid-February:

  • Sable Offshore (SOC) 23.41%
  • New Fortress Energy (NFE) 19.56%
  • Green Plains (GPRE) 17.51%
  • Gevo (GEVO) 14%
  • New Era Energy & Digital (NUAI) 13.85%

Least shorted energy stocks as of mid-February:

  • Vantage (VNTG) 0.50%
  • Drilling Tools International (DTI) 0.50%
  • North European Oil Royalty Trust (NRT) 0.53%
  • Marine Petroleum Trust (MARPS) 0.56%
  • Verde Clean Fuels (VGAS) 0.57%

data.title: EQT targets $3.5B in 2026 free cash flow with expansion in midstream and infrastructure investments
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: EQT Corporation (EQT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Toby Rice opened by emphasizing "2025 was another stellar year for EQT, one in which we were able to clearly demonstrate the power of our platform" and highlighted the company's focus on operational excellence, financial strength, and scale. Rice stated, "Production consistently topped expectations throughout 2025, driven by compression project outperformance and robust well productivity."
  • Rice noted, "The cumulative benefits of our marketing optimization resulted in more than $200 million of free cash flow uplift last year relative to guidance." He described EQT's position as "the second largest marketer of natural gas in the U.S. ahead of all upstream and midstream peers."
  • Rice announced the decision to "exercise our option to purchase additional interest in MVP Mainline and MVP Boost from an affiliate of Con Edison... Upon close, our ownership in MVP Mainline and MVP Boost will increase to approximately 53%." He explained the purchase price equates to "roughly 9x adjusted EBITDA and delivers a low-risk 12% IRR to EQT."
  • Rice unveiled the 2026 plan: "We are initiating a production forecast of 2.275 to 2.375 Tcfe... and are allocating the first $600 million of post-dividend free cash flow to these projects in 2026."
  • CFO Jeremy Knop stated, "Our strong execution resulted in nearly $750 million of free cash flow attributable to EQT in the fourth quarter, approximately $200 million above consensus expectations. This marks the sixth quarter in a row where we have exceeded consensus free cash flow estimates with an average beat of 40%."
  • Knop highlighted, "We exited the year with net debt of just under $7.7 billion, inclusive of $425 million of working capital usage during the quarter."

Outlook

  • Rice projected, "At recent strip pricing, we expect to generate 2026 adjusted EBITDA attributable to EQT of approximately $6.5 billion and 2026 free cash flow attributable to EQT of $3.5 billion, which includes the impact of approximately $600 million in growth investments."
  • Rice stated, "Cumulative free cash flow attributable to EQT over the next 5 years is projected to total more than $16 billion."
  • Rice indicated a maintenance capital budget of "$2.07 billion to $2.21 billion, which includes a full year impact from the Olympus acquisition."
  • Management described a continuation of "allocating the first dollars of post-dividend free cash flow to these projects in 2026."

Financial Results

  • Rice reported, "Efficiency gains resulted in our average 2025 well cost per lateral foot coming in 13% lower year-over-year and 6% below our internal forecast coming into the year."
  • Rice noted, "Per unit LOE was nearly 15% below our expectations last year and approximately 50% lower than the peer average."
  • Rice stated, "The cumulative result of our operational outperformance delivered $2.5 billion of free cash flow attributable to EQT in 2025 with NYMEX natural gas prices averaging approximately $3.40 per million Btu for the year."
  • Knop added, "Recent gas price strength on the back of winter Storm Fern, combined with our opportunistic approach to hedging should drive historic results for EQT in the first quarter, with the potential for free cash flow in the month of February alone to approach $1 billion."
  • Knop described a hedge position for 2026: "We are now nearly 40% hedged in the first quarter of 2026 with an average floor price of roughly $4.30 per MMBtu and an average ceiling of $6.30."

Q&A

  • Douglas George Blyth Leggate, Wolfe Research: Asked about portfolio breakeven, sustaining capital, and free cash flow priorities. Rice responded, "The deleveraging has been rapid... leverage is still the priority. We'll continue to expand on that."
  • Neil Mehta, Goldman Sachs: Inquired about performance during Winter Storm Fern and the company's ability to manage volatility. Rice explained, "Volatility is here in natural gas... we are trying to take advantage of volatility, and we think the results that we generated in winter storm fern are going to be future opportunities."
  • Arun Jayaram, JPMorgan: Asked about growth CapEx and project pipeline. Knop replied, "Right now, the focus is really on the Mountain Valley projects... beyond what we have in the queue for this year, the focus is really on... Southgate and Boost."
  • Wei Jiang, Barclays: Asked about gas sales strategy and market exposure. Knop described EQT's approach: "We try to always have a view... what we're really trying to understand is where the asymmetry sit and the potential outcome."
  • Phillip Jungwirth, BMO: Asked about LNG offtake and international buyer interest. Knop stated, "I would say... demand is a lot more real than people realize."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed management on capital allocation, growth, and risk management, with a tone that was neutral to slightly positive, seeking clarity on free cash flow deployment and growth timing.
  • Management displayed confidence, with Rice stating, "We are only getting started and the momentum we've built across the organization gives us tremendous confidence in what lies ahead for EQT." Prepared remarks were highly optimistic; responses in Q&A remained disciplined and detailed, with emphasis on prudence and strategic timing.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone from both sides remained confident, but management showed increased assertiveness regarding both operational and financial outperformance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw a step-up in guidance detail, particularly with a projected $3.5 billion in 2026 free cash flow and an explicit five-year free cash flow projection.
  • Management increased focus on infrastructure and midstream investments, highlighting the MVP Boost project and new pipeline developments, compared to broader strategic commentary in the prior quarter.
  • Analysts' questions remained focused on growth, capital allocation, and market volatility, similar to the previous quarter, but with heightened attention on near-term free cash flow and infrastructure returns.
  • Management's confidence and emphasis on recurring operational outperformance and marketing optimization were more pronounced than in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited structural constraints in U.S. natural gas infrastructure, with Rice warning, "These price signals are unmistakable. The country needs more pipeline infrastructure and a permitting framework that allows the industry to get back to building critical infrastructure again."
  • Knop noted, "The natural gas market has tightened significantly over the past 6 weeks... inventories below the 5-year average."
  • Management discussed volatility, emphasizing the necessity of market optimization and the ability to respond quickly to market shifts and extreme weather events.
  • Analysts raised concerns about timing for growth, market share, and the risk of chasing price signals.

Final Takeaway

Management underscored another year of operational and financial outperformance, building on EQT's integrated platform to deliver record free cash flow and significant debt reduction. Strategic investments in midstream and infrastructure, combined with a disciplined approach to maintenance capital and targeted growth, position EQT to capture value from rising demand and market volatility. With a robust project pipeline and continued operational efficiencies, EQT projects strong free cash flow generation and remains focused on compounding shareholder value through prudent capital allocation and opportunistic growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Meta to spend $65M in 2026 to boost politicians who favor AI - report
data.description:
Meta is getting ready to spend $65M this year to boost state politicians who favor the artificial intelligence industry, The New York Times reported on Wednesday, citing company representatives.

The sum is "the biggest election investment" by Meta, the report said.

Meta is betting bigger on politics, driven by concerns over the regulatory threat to the artificial intelligence industry as it aims to beat back legislation in states that it fears could inhibit AI development, company representatives told NYT.

To accomplish that, Meta is quietly starting two new super PACs, according to federal filings surfaced by The New York Times. One group, Forge the Future Project, is backing Republicans. Another, Making Our Tomorrow, is backing Democrats.

The new PACs join two others, the American Technology Excellence Project and Mobilizing Economic Transformation Across California, already started by Meta. In total, the four super PACs have an initial budget of $65M, according to federal and state filings.

Meta’s spending is set to start this week in Illinois and Texas, where the company generally favors backing Democratic and Republican incumbents or engaging in open races rather than deposing existing officials, company representatives told NYT in interviews.

Last year, Meta’s public policy vice president, Brian Rice, said the company would start spending in politics because of "inconsistent regulations that threaten homegrown innovation and investments in AI."

Meta put $45M into the American Technology Excellence Project in September. That money is expected, in turn, to flow to Forge the Future Project, Making Our Tomorrow, and potentially to other entities.

In August, Meta put $20M into Mobilizing Economic Transformation Across California. California, which has some of the country’s most onerous campaign finance disclosures, requires the sponsoring company to be disclosed in the name of the entity.

In December, Meta put $5M into another California committee called California Leads, which is focused on promoting moderate business policy and not AI, according to state records.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Liberty Global outlines Ziggo Group spin-off and targets $1.5B corporate cash by 2026 amid major UK/Netherlands transactions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Liberty Global (LBTYA) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Michael Fries, Vice Chairman, President, CEO & Chairman of the Board, highlighted two major new transactions: the acquisition of Vodafone's 50% stake in VodafoneZiggo and the intention to list and spin off the new Ziggo Group, combining Dutch and Belgian operations. Fries stated, “We announced 2 significant transactions earlier today...this is exactly what we said we would do on our call last year and the year before.”
  • Fries reported that Liberty Telecom’s four national FMC champions generated $22 billion of revenue and $8 billion of EBITDA on an aggregate basis. He also noted a 75% reduction in net corporate spend over the last 12 months.
  • Fries commented on operational momentum, especially in the second half of the year, with “all of our 3 large OpCos hit their guidance targets last year.” He emphasized continued capital allocation discipline, referencing a reduction in share buybacks from 10% to 5% and current opportunistic stances.
  • Charles Bracken, Executive VP & CFO, stated, “Our operating companies in the U.K., the Netherlands and Belgium delivered on their full year guidance metrics despite challenging market conditions.” Bracken further explained, “We have fully refinanced all 2028 maturities following successful term loan refinancings, senior secured note issuances and private taps within these credit silos.”
  • Bracken announced the introduction of a 1.5% annual management fee of assets under management paid by Liberty Growth to Liberty Services starting January 2026, funded by distributions from the growth portfolio.

Outlook

  • For Virgin Media O2 (VMO2), revenue guidance for 2026 is a 3% to 5% decline in total service revenues, with adjusted EBITDA also expected to decline by 3% to 5%. “Adjusted free cash flow of around GBP 200 million for the year, supporting cash distributions to shareholders of the same amount,” stated Bracken.
  • VodafoneZiggo guidance indicates a stable to low single-digit revenue decline and mid- to high single-digit decline in adjusted EBITDA due to OpEx investments into network resilience and service reliability. Adjusted free cash flow is expected to be around EUR 100 million with no shareholder distributions planned for the year.
  • Telenet’s 2026 guidance projects stable revenue growth and low single-digit growth in adjusted EBITDAaL, with positive adjusted free cash flow of around EUR 20 million.
  • Bracken expects Liberty Corporate to have around $50 million negative adjusted EBITDA in 2026.

Financial Results

  • Bracken reported, “VMO2 delivered a revenue decline of 5.9% on a reported basis, which was impacted by lower Nexfibre construction revenues due to a slowdown in the fiber build and also sustained competitive pressure in both the fixed and mobile market in the U.K.” Adjusted EBITDA for VMO2 declined by 2.4% on a reported basis.
  • VodafoneZiggo revenue declined by 2.3% in Q4, with adjusted EBITDA down 3.4%, attributed to lower revenue and higher costs from commercial initiatives.
  • Telenet revenue fell by 1.3%, primarily due to not renewing Belgium football broadcasting rights, while adjusted EBITDA dropped by 9.9% due to increased labor and marketing costs.
  • Pro forma for the announced transactions and expected asset sales, Liberty Global anticipates ending 2026 with $1.5 billion in cash.

Q&A

  • Robert Grindle, Deutsche Bank AG: Asked if reaching 8 million Nexfibre homes post-deal definitively unlocks the UK wholesale opportunity. Fries responded, “The 8 million will be achieved relatively quickly end of '27 probably...first order of business is to grow and manage our own customer base on that 20 million home network, but also very much so to provide a wholesale opportunity for the market.”
  • Joshua Mills, BNP Paribas: Inquired about synergies and deleveraging in the Ziggo Group combination. Fries explained, “There is no strategy or plan to build fiber in the Netherlands, and we don't believe it's necessary either from a commercial and certainly not attractive from a capital point of view.” Bracken clarified, “That $500 million is the annual target. It's not a cumulative target.”
  • Polo Tang, UBS: Questioned VMO2’s 2026 guidance and specifics of the Netomnia Nexfibre deal. Lutz Schüler, CEO (Leave of Absence), replied, “You can broadly contribute 30% to the B2B restatement of numbers, including Daisy. And 70% is attributed to a cautious view on the fixed consumer market.” Andrea Salvato, Chief Development Officer, clarified there are no minimum penetration or migration commitments in the transaction.
  • David Wright, BofA Securities: Asked about the Ziggo spin-off’s equity story and required synergy run rate. Fries answered, “We believe 2027...we are going to be able to see or forecast the kind of storyline here that the market will want to see. That does reflect and has comparisons to Sunrise, namely a deleveraging story from free cash flow, EBITDA growth and asset sales.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed skepticism and sought granular clarity on deal structure, guidance reliability, and the sustainability of synergies, with questions often focused on risk, investment timing, and competitive dynamics. Tone was neutral-to-slightly negative, especially regarding the outlook for VMO2 and Ziggo.
  • Management’s prepared remarks were confident, emphasizing “we are serious about delivering value to shareholders” and “our structure today is fit for purpose.” During Q&A, responses were largely detailed but occasionally hedged, with phrases like “we have lots of flexibility here, tons of freedom to plan how and when and what we do.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, there was greater emphasis on strategic transactions and restructuring, with management showing continued optimism but fielding more probing analyst questions regarding transaction details and forward guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter’s call was dominated by the announcement of two major transactions: the acquisition of Vodafone’s stake in VodafoneZiggo and the plan to spin off Ziggo Group. This represents a shift from the previous quarter’s focus on operational momentum and cost management.
  • Management provided more granular guidance by operating company, introducing new reporting structures and disclosure for VMO2 post-O2 Daisy creation. There was a pronounced focus on proactive capital allocation, asset sales, and deleveraging.
  • Analysts in both quarters pressed on competitive dynamics and capital efficiency, but this quarter’s Q&A displayed heightened scrutiny of transaction structures and synergy realization.
  • The tone from management remained confident, though more defensive when pressed on specifics of new deals and guidance projections compared to the previous quarter’s forward-looking optimism about operational improvements.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged challenging market conditions, particularly sustained competition in the U.K. fixed and mobile markets and the impact on VMO2’s results.
  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of synergy targets in cross-border deals and the ability to realize operational benefits without substantial new CapEx.
  • Bracken addressed concerns about Telenet refinancing, stating, “We still have plenty of time...as these steps unfold, we can essentially reprice the debt and extend the maturity.”
  • Uncertainties around the U.K. market’s competitive intensity and future wholesale pricing commitments were highlighted, with management indicating that some details would not be disclosed at this stage.

Final Takeaway

Liberty Global’s fourth quarter call marked a strategic pivot with the announcement of the Ziggo Group spin-off and substantial UK/Netherlands fiber transactions, alongside detailed 2026 guidance and a disciplined approach to capital allocation and deleveraging. Management emphasized that these moves align with previously communicated strategic priorities, focusing on unlocking shareholder value, reducing corporate spend, and positioning the telecom and growth portfolios for future cash generation and operational resilience. The company signaled confidence in its ability to execute, while analysts remained focused on execution risks and the realization of planned synergies.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: ICON rises as Cowen upgrades after recent selloff
data.description:
Shares of ICON Public Limited (ICLR) gained on Wednesday after TD Cowen upgraded the contract research organization to Buy from Hold, noting a compelling risk-reward setup following its recent selloff.

Ireland-based ICON (ICLR) lost nearly 40% of its market value on Thursday after pulling its 2025 outlook in reaction to an ongoing investigation into some of its accounting practices.

The company also said that its 2023 and 2024 revenue figures may have been overstated by less than 2% due to potential “material weaknesses” in certain internal controls.

“We believe share reaction to ICLR’s investigation into accounting practices overstates the anticipated impact to earnings,” TD Cowen analyst Charles Rhyee wrote. He added that while risks related to the ongoing probe and AI concerns remain, "we find risk/reward compelling.”

Rhyee also pointed out that ICON (ICLR) continues to generate meaningful free cash flow and said that the company’s decision to withdraw its outlook is overdone and doesn’t reflect the projected impact on earnings.

However, the analyst cut his price target on the stock to $120 from $183 per share, implying an approximately 35% upside to ICLR’s closing price on Tuesday.

data.title: Travel+Leisure Q4 results, guidance, and dividend hike fuel record high
data.description:
Better than expected results for the final quarter of the year, a new share repurchase agreement, and increased dividend launched shares of Travel + Leisure (TNL) to a record high on Wednesday with a gain of as much as 11%.

“Travel + Leisure’s 2025 results demonstrate the consistency and resilience of our performance, led by sustained momentum in our core Vacation Ownership business...and as we begin 2026, leisure travel demand remains strong, reinforcing our confidence in the durability of our business,” said CEO Michael D. Brown.

 An 8% increase in vacation ownership revenue helped offset declining sales in its travel and membership segment, resulting in net revenue of $1.03B, a 5.7% increase from a year ago and $30M better than expected.

The travel company’s profitability also improved, with a key operating income metric increasing 8% to $272M and adjusted EPS up 6% to $1.83, beating estimates by a penny.

For the current quarter, Travel+Leisure (TNL) sees adjusted EBITDA to be between $210M and $220M, an increase of 6% at the midpoint over last year. Gross vacation ownership interest ((VOI)) sales are expected to be between $520M and $540M, while volume per guest ((VPG)) is expected to be between $3,200 and $3,250. 

For the full year, adjusted EBITDA is forecasted to increase to $1.03B and $1.055B from $990M in FY25.

In addition to upbeat quarterly results, Travel+Leisure (TNL) recommended a 7% increase to its quarterly dividend to $0.60 per share and approved a new $750M share repurchase plan.

data.title: Most and least shorted large-cap energy stocks as of mid-Feb
data.description:
By mid-February, the S&P 500 energy sector (XLE) moved 22% in the upward direction this year supported by a 13% rise in crude oil futures (CL1:COM) over potential disruption in global crude supply.

Against this backdrop, short interest trends among large-cap energy stocks show a wide divergence. Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) was the most shorted stock in January, while Cheniere Energy Partners (CQP) was the least shorted stock.

Most shorted energy stocks as of mid-February:

  • Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) 25.81%
  • Centrus Energy (LEU) 24.12%
  • Delek US Holdings (DK) 20.25%
  • Northern Oil and Gas (NOG) 19.43%
  • PBF Energy (PBF) 16.44%
 
 

Least shorted energy stocks as of mid-February:

  • MPLX (MPLX) 0.72%, unchanged from last month.
  • Alliance Resource Partners (ARLP) 0.90%
  • Energy Transfer (ET) 0.92%
  • Mach Natural Resources (MNR) 0.98%
